commit.message:
fix: make ValidationError inherit from ValueError for backward compatibility

Integration tests for delegation chain depth use pytest.raises(ValueError).
Task 9.3 changed the chain_depth_exceeded raise from ValueError to
ValidationError to carry structured detail (chain_depth_remaining,
effective_max_chain_depth). Making ValidationError a subclass of both
AgentAuthError and ValueError satisfies both: the API layer gets the
structured detail dict, and callers catching ValueError still work.

commit.message:
fix: Windows supervisor dies on SSH session exit (#7)

* fix: add CREATE_BREAKAWAY_FROM_JOB for Windows session detachment

On Windows, when bgtask is launched via SSH (e.g. OpenSSH server),
the supervisor process was killed when the SSH session ended. This
happened because the SSH server places child processes in a Job Object,
and when the session closes, all processes in the job are terminated.

Adding CREATE_BREAKAWAY_FROM_JOB to the process creation flags allows
the supervisor to break out of the session's job object, surviving
SSH disconnects.

If the parent job doesn't allow breakaway (ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED), fall
back to the previous behavior without the flag — the supervisor will
start but may not survive session exit.

Also replaced raw hex constants with named constants from x/sys/windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract detach helper and surface breakaway fallback warning

- Extract newDetachedCmd() to deduplicate command setup between main
  and fallback paths in Detach (Windows)
- Return bool from Detach indicating breakaway failure so callers can
  warn that the supervisor may not survive session exit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: fix parallel test flakiness and bash 3.2 empty-array expansion

- Fix 'bats_opts[@]: unbound variable' under set -u + bash 3.2: empty
  arrays must use ${arr[@]+"${arr[@]}"} idiom, not "${arr[@]}"
- Split core_performance.bats out of the parallel batch; run it after
  all parallel workers finish so wall-clock timing assertions aren't
  skewed by CPU contention from concurrent bats processes
- Raise MOLE_PERF_GET_INVOKING_USER_LIMIT_MS default 500→2000ms and
  add MOLE_PERF_SECTION_LIMIT_MS (default 2000ms) to give sufficient
  headroom without masking real regressions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow (#154)

* chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow

Manual workflow_dispatch that rolls back the VPS to any previously
released image version. Accepts a version string (e.g. 0.12.4 or v0.12.4),
strips the leading 'v' to match the Docker image tag convention, pulls
the pinned image on the VPS, retags it as :latest, and restarts via
docker compose — without touching .env or docker-compose.yml.

Shares the deploy-vps concurrency group to prevent concurrent
deploy/rollback races. Guarded to main branch only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add deploy-vps concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml

The rollback workflow used group: deploy-vps but no existing deploy
workflow shared that group, so concurrent deploy + rollback runs were
not actually blocked.

Adding the same concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml (the reusable
workflow called by both deploy.yml and release.yml) means all VPS
deployments now share the group with rollback.yml, enforcing real
mutual exclusion.

Addresses PR review comment: concurrency group not shared with existing deploy workflows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Rename misleading input_rank param to input_dim_value in processSlice… (#7739)

This pull request improves the handling of the `Slice` operator when
applied to empty or scalar-shaped tensors, ensuring correct shape
inference and preventing potential crashes. The changes include both a
bug fix in the shape inference logic and the addition of a targeted test
case.

**Bug fix for `Slice` shape inference:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to use the
actual input dimension size or value (instead of input rank) when
calculating slice bounds, ensuring correct handling for empty or scalar
dimensions.

**Testing improvements:**

* Added a new test `test_slice_scalar_shape_output` in
`shape_inference_test.py` to verify that slicing the shape of a scalar
tensor produces a zero-length output and does not crash.

---------

Signed-off-by: Ti-Tai Wang <titaiwang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(purge): remove 60-char hard cap on path display width (issue #564)

The path column in 'mo purge' selection was capped at 60 characters
regardless of terminal width. On wide terminals (120+ cols) this caused
long project paths like ~/GitHub/Ulama/transformer-project to be
truncated unnecessarily to ~17 visible characters.

Remove the hard 60-char cap and let the available terminal space be the
only upper bound, which is already computed as:
  available_for_path = terminal_width - fixed_overhead

Paths now use as much space as the terminal allows while still keeping
the size and artifact-type columns readable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(analyze): add darwin build tag to all platform-specific files

All files in cmd/analyze/ are macOS-only but several were missing the
//go:build darwin constraint. On Linux (e.g. CodeQL CI), Go compiled
these files without the types defined in main.go (which had the tag),
causing undefined symbol errors for dirEntry, fileEntry, scanResult,
cacheEntry, historyEntry and model.

- Add //go:build darwin to heap.go, cache.go, scanner.go, cleanable.go,
  delete.go, format.go, constants.go and all *_test.go files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement TODO 24 - RUNTIME MINIMAL for KOTLIN86

Add EmitMinimalRuntime procedure with 7 runtime functions:
- _KTRT_PRINTLN: print string + newline
- _KTRT_ABS: absolute value
- _KTRT_MAX/_KTRT_MIN: max/min of two values
- _KTRT_ALLOC/_KTRT_NEW: static heap allocation (4096-byte static heap)
- _KTRT_TYPECODE: read object typecode
- _KTRT_CHKIDX: array bounds checking (reuses existing _KT_IOOB_MSG)

[CI] Add manifest diff workflow (Part 2/3) (#3301)

## Motivation

Add a reusable GitHub Actions workflow for generating manifest diff
reports on-demand. This workflow enables both manual triggering and
integration with other CI workflows.

[**Example
Report**](https://therock-ci-artifacts.s3.amazonaws.com/23025588012-linux/logs/manifest-diff/TheRockReport.html)

This is Part 2/3 of the manifest diff report feature (split from #2161).
- Part 1: #3295 - Python scripts and tests
- Part 2: This PR - Reusable workflow
- Part 3: CI nightly integration (depends on this PR)

## Technical details

- **`.github/workflows/manifest-diff.yml`**
  - `workflow_dispatch` for manual runs (GitHub UI or CLI).
  - `workflow_call` for use by other workflows (e.g. `ci_nightly.yml`).
- Single “Generate manifest diff report” step: all inputs are passed
through to the script; the script validates combinations and reports
clear errors for invalid input.
- Supports the same four input combinations (see below); validation and
behavior live in the script, not in workflow conditionals.
- S3 upload of the generated HTML report via
`upload_test_report_script.py` (when not from a fork).

- **`build_tools/github_actions/upload_test_report_script.py`**
- Appends the S3 report URL to the job step summary via
`gha_append_step_summary` so the report link appears in the Actions UI.
Safe when not in GHA (no-op if `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` is unset).

- **Script and template (supporting changes)**  
- Script: optional args from the workflow (e.g. empty `--start` /
`--find-last-successful`) are normalized and validated so invalid
combinations fail with a clear message.
- Template: commit range arrow and section icons use HTML entities and
`<meta charset="utf-8">` so the report displays correctly and avoids
encoding issues (e.g. mojibake in the commit range line).


Input combinations supported:
- `find_last_successful` + `workflow_mode`: Find start from last
successful, end as workflow ID
- `find_last_successful` only: Find start from last successful, end as
commit SHA
- `workflow_mode` only: Both start and end as workflow IDs
- Neither: Both start and end as commit SHAs

## Test Plan

- [x] Trigger workflow manually with
`find_last_successful=ci_nightly.yml` and hardcoded parameters
- [x] Verify report is generated and uploaded to S3
- [x] Verify step summary appears in GitHub Actions UI

## Test Result

workflow
mode)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024165253/job/66867809624#step:3:2)
Test Case 2: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 2 (find-last-successful, end
as
SHA)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024353822/job/66868457526#step:3:2)
Test Case 3: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 3 (workflow mode, start/end
as run
IDs)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024506197/job/66868997210#step:3:2)
Test Case 4: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 4 (start/end as commit
SHAs)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024684367/job/66869608549#step:3:2)

## Submission Checklist

- [ ] Look over the contributing guidelines at
https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.

feat: Add DocGuard CDD enforcement extension to community catalog (#1838)

* feat: add DocGuard CDD enforcement extension to community catalog

DocGuard is a Canonical-Driven Development enforcement tool that generates,
validates, scores, and traces project documentation against 51 automated checks.

Provides 6 commands:
- guard: 51-check validation with quality labels
- diagnose: AI-ready fix prompts
- score: CDD maturity scoring (0-100)
- trace: ISO 29119 traceability matrix
- generate: Reverse-engineer docs from codebase
- init: Initialize CDD with compliance profiles

Features:
- Zero dependencies (pure Node.js)
- Config-aware traceability (respects .docguard.json)
- Orphan file detection
- Research-backed (AITPG/TRACE, IEEE TSE/TMLCN 2026)

npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/docguard-cli
GitHub: https://github.com/raccioly/docguard

The source archive URL nests files under a subdirectory, so the
Spec Kit installer cannot find extension.yml at the archive root.
Switch to a release asset ZIP built from the extension directory.

commit.message:
FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts to fix scene_texture crash (#380)

* Initial plan

* FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts for proper GL isolation

Fixes _mesa_ReadPixels crash in SoSceneTexture2P::updatePBuffer when the
Rendering/scene_texture demo is shown in the obol_viewer System GL panel.

Root cause: SoOffscreenRenderer sets SORTED_OBJECT_BLEND transparency which
forces updatePBuffer (not updateFrameBuffer). updatePBuffer needs real GL
context isolation — it assumes makeContextCurrent switches to a fresh inner
context so that glReadPixels sees only tmpFbo, not the outer renderer's FBO.
The old FLTKContextManager shared one GL context for everything, so the outer
FBO binding leaked into the inner "context", causing the Mesa crash.

Fix (X11/GLX): ensureWindow() now records mainGLCtx_ and mainDrawable_ after
the first make_current(). createOffscreenContext() creates a real GLXContext
via glXCreateContext(dpy, vi, mainGLCtx_, True) — sharing textures/VBOs with
the FLTK context but having isolated GL state. makeContextCurrent() uses
glXMakeCurrent() to actually switch; restorePreviousContext() switches back.
Windows/macOS fall back to the original single-context approach (unchanged).

Co-authored-by: starseeker <238416+starseeker@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: starseeker <238416+starseeker@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix shortcut to target pythonw.exe directly for taskbar pinning

Windows only allows pinning shortcuts to .exe targets. Changed from
run.vbs to pointing directly at .venv\Scripts\pythonw.exe with
src\main.py as argument. Now right-click -> Pin to taskbar works.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add one-click launcher with taskbar pinning support

- run.bat: activates venv + launches with pythonw.exe (no console)
- run.vbs: silent wrapper that hides even the batch window flash
- assets/icon.ico: multi-size app icon (256/64/48/32/16px)
- create-shortcut.ps1: creates Desktop shortcut with icon, pinnable

Usage: powershell -File create-shortcut.ps1, then pin to taskbar

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement mini x86 assembler for PE binary generation

Replace the 2-byte stub (INT3+RET) in BinWriteExe with a full mini
x86 assembler that reads the generated ASM file and assembles actual
machine code into BinCode[]/BinData[] buffers.

New procedures:
- BinAssemble: reads .ASM file, dispatches to encoders
- BinResolveFixups: resolves REL32/ABS32/IAT32 fixups
- AsmEncodeInstr: encodes all x86 instruction patterns
- AsmEncodeData/AsmEncodeCodeData: encodes data directives
- Helper functions for register/operand/memory parsing

Modified BinWriteExe: removed stub, added fixup resolution
Modified Run: always generates temp ASM, calls BinAssemble

All 15 ABAP test files produce valid PE executables with
2238-3582 bytes of code instead of the old 2-byte stub.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[#176] Replace lightbox with Spotlight.js for docs image lightbox (#330)

Implements #176.

The original lightbox2 library renders SVG images oversized. This PR
replaces it with
[Spotlight.js](https://github.com/nextapps-de/spotlight), a lightweight,
dependency-free modern lightbox library.

## Summary

- Replace `sphinxcontrib-lightbox2` with a new `sphinxcontrib-spotlight`
Sphinx extension
- The extension bundles Spotlight.js v0.7.8 (single 22KB bundle, vs 70KB
for glightbox)
- All documentation images are automatically wrapped in `<a
class="spotlight">` anchors via a Sphinx post-transform
- Images can be excluded per-image with the `no-spotlight` CSS class

## Changes

**New package** `py_packages/sphinxcontrib_spotlight/`:
- Bundles Spotlight.js v0.7.8 as a single self-contained JS bundle
(includes CSS)
- Custom `spotlight_reference` node defers URI resolution to write time,
ensuring hrefs point to `_images/` output paths rather than source paths
- `SpotlightTransform` post-transform wraps all `image` nodes
automatically
- Alt text becomes `data-description` for the lightbox caption
- Spotlight auto-initializes from markup — no manual JS init script
needed

**Updated configuration:**
- `docs/source/conf.py`: switched extension from
`sphinxcontrib.lightbox2` → `sphinxcontrib.spotlight`, removed
`lightbox2_*` config options
- `pyproject.toml`: updated dependency and uv editable source

## Testing

- [x] Sphinx build completes without errors
- [x] Extension loads correctly in the docs venv
(`sphinxcontrib.spotlight` importable)
- [x] Old `sphinxcontrib-glightbox` and `sphinxcontrib-lightbox2`
packages removed from venv

---------

Signed-off-by: Anton Matosov <anton.matosov@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix falsy enum values returning None instead of name

AlarmReason.NO_ALARM has value 0, making it falsy in Python. The
_enum_to_lowercase function used 'if enum_value' which incorrectly
returned None for NO_ALARM. Changed to 'if enum_value is not None'
to properly handle zero-valued Flag enums.

This caused battery_monitor alarm and dc_energy_meter alarm to show
as 'unknown' instead of 'no_alarm' in Home Assistant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Slice shape inference crashes with SIGABRT on empty dimensions (#7738)

Fix #7735 

This pull request addresses issues with the ONNX Slice operator when
handling empty dimensions, ensuring correct and safe shape inference in
these edge cases. The main focus is to prevent undefined behavior and
ensure that slicing on empty dimensions consistently produces outputs
with zero length, regardless of the step direction. Additional
regression tests are included to verify these fixes, and several test
data files are updated to reflect the new behavior.

**Slice operator improvements for empty dimensions:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to
short-circuit and produce a zero-length output when the input dimension
size is zero, avoiding invalid clamp bounds and potential undefined
behavior.
* Modified shape inference logic in `old.cc` to detect empty dimensions
and set the output dimension value to zero, both for standard and
expanded shape inference code paths.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR2635-R2645)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR6329-R6335)

**Testing and regression coverage:**

* Added two new tests in `shape_inference_test.py` to verify that
slicing on empty dimensions with both positive and negative steps
produces the correct output shape, preventing regression of issue #7735.

**Test data updates:**

* Updated several `output_0.pb` binary test data files under
`onnx/backend/test/data/node/` (including attention, gelu,
hammingwindow, and lppool tests) to align with the corrected Slice
operator behavior on empty dimensions.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-875055c843db41ea7d579c8bd29201e6f60bb4175ac96f840bdd27d488866198L1-R7)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d6fe5631baa8b6cb320c03143f797abe3dce4ddf5363fa9cfe71cc12bc31ac53L2-R3)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-3275d5e3517a0e95967de3c46a75b02104980f2566f1a0cbefa386ea8e02f312L1-R3)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-537fe44987ef880d522c9c88283070745874abc990951f49eff8dccc35630af1L2-R2)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3d57e2f0cf63b32349f6619a2f61e90d4a4aa4fdac9a794af6bfb00c97fe9f8eL1-R2)

These changes collectively improve the robustness of ONNX shape
inference and operator correctness when handling tensors with empty
dimensions.

---------

Signed-off-by: Ti-Tai Wang <titaiwang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(presets): Pluggable preset system with catalog, resolver, and skills propagation (#1787)

* Initial plan

* feat(templates): add pluggable template system with packs, catalog, resolver, and CLI commands

Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(templates): add comprehensive unit tests for template pack system

Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(presets): pluggable preset system with template/command overrides, catalog, and resolver

- Rename 'template packs' to 'presets' to avoid naming collision with core templates
- PresetManifest, PresetRegistry, PresetManager, PresetCatalog, PresetResolver in presets.py
- Extract CommandRegistrar to agents.py as shared infrastructure
- CLI: specify preset list/add/remove/search/resolve/info
- CLI: specify preset catalog list/add/remove
- --preset option on specify init
- Priority-based preset stacking (--priority, lower = higher precedence)
- Command overrides registered into all detected agent directories (17+ agents)
- Extension command safety: skip registration if target extension not installed
- Multi-catalog support: env var, project config, user config, built-in defaults
- resolve_template() / Resolve-Template in bash/PowerShell scripts
- Self-test preset: overrides all 6 core templates + 1 command
- Scaffold with 4 examples: core/extension template and command overrides
- Preset catalog (catalog.json, catalog.community.json)
- Documentation: README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, PUBLISHING.md
- 110 preset tests, 253 total tests passing

* feat(presets): propagate command overrides to skills via init-options

- Add save_init_options() / load_init_options() helpers that persist
  CLI flags from 'specify init' to .specify/init-options.json
- PresetManager._register_skills() overwrites SKILL.md files when
  --ai-skills was used during init and corresponding skill dirs exist
- PresetManager._unregister_skills() restores core template content
  on preset removal
- registered_skills stored in preset registry metadata
- 8 new tests covering skill override, skip conditions, and restore

* fix: address PR check failures (ruff F541, CodeQL URL substring)

- Remove extraneous f-prefix from two f-strings without placeholders
- Replace substring URL check in test with startswith/endswith assertions
  to satisfy CodeQL incomplete URL substring sanitization rule

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments

- Move save_init_options() before preset install so skills propagation
  works during 'specify init --preset --ai-skills'
- Clean up downloaded ZIP after successful preset install during init
- Validate --from URL scheme (require HTTPS, HTTP only for localhost)
- Expose unregister_commands() on extensions.py CommandRegistrar wrapper
  instead of reaching into private _registrar field
- Use _get_merged_packs() for search() and get_pack_info() so all
  active catalogs are searched, not just the highest-priority one
- Fix fetch_catalog() cache to verify cached URL matches current URL
- Fix PresetResolver: script resolution uses .sh extension, consistent
  file extensions throughout resolve(), and resolve_with_source()
  delegates to resolve() to honor template_type parameter
- Fix bash common.sh: fall through to directory scan when python3
  returns empty preset list
- Fix PowerShell Resolve-Template: filter out dot-folders and sort
  extensions deterministically

* fix: narrow empty except blocks and add explanatory comments

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments (round 2)

- Fix init --preset error masking: distinguish "not found" from real errors
- Fix bash resolve_template: skip hidden dirs in extensions (match Python/PS)
- Fix temp dir leaks in tests: use temp_dir fixture instead of mkdtemp
- Fix self-test catalog entry: add note that it's local-only (no download_url)
- Fix Windows path issue in resolve_with_source: use Path.relative_to()
- Fix skill restore path: use project's .specify/templates/commands/ not source tree
- Add encoding="utf-8" to all file read/write in agents.py
- Update test to set up core command templates for skill restoration

* fix: remove self-test from catalog.json (local-only preset)

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments (round 3)

- Fix PS Resolve-Template fallback to skip dot-prefixed dirs (.cache)
- Rename _catalog to _catalog_name for consistency with extension system
- Enforce install_allowed policy in CLI preset add and download_pack()
- Fix shell injection: pass registry path via env var instead of string interpolation

* fix: correct PresetError docstring from template to preset

* Removed CHANGELOG requirement

* Applying review recommendations

* Applying review recommendations

* Applying review recommendations

* Applying review recommendations

---------

- Add src/lib/types.ts with TypeScript domain types
- Add src/lib/query-keys.ts with TanStack Query key factory
- Create API routes: evaluari CRUD, riscuri CRUD, duplicate, export stub, templates
- Add React Query hooks: use-evaluari, use-riscuri, use-templates
- Add QueryProvider client component
- Update root layout with QueryProvider, Romanian metadata
- Create Header layout component with navy branding
- Create DashboardPage feature component

- Create RiscCard, RiscFormModal components for risk management

feat: implement Phase 9 Agent DX improvements (tasks 9.1-9.4)

Task 9.1 - Configurable Access Token Lifetime per Credential:
- Add token_lifetime_seconds column to Credential model
- Add token_lifetime_seconds field to CredentialCreate (ge=60, le=86400)
- Surface token_lifetime_seconds in CredentialResponse
- Use custom lifetime when minting tokens via client_credentials grant
- Alembic migration: add_token_lifetime_seconds_to_credentials

Task 9.2 - Surface Quickstart Scope Constraints via Discover and Errors:
- Add 'quickstart' key to /.well-known/agent-configuration with description,
  scopes:[], and next_steps
- Add 'hint' field to invalid_scope error when credential has empty scopes

Task 9.3 - Make Delegation Chain Depth Semantics Explicit:
- Add chain_depth_remaining to DelegationResponse (max_chain_depth - chain_depth)
- Add max_absolute_chain_depth alias to DelegationResponse
- Add 'delegation' section to /discover with depth_semantics and description
- Improve chain-depth-exceeded error with structured detail fields

Task 9.4 - Emit Token Expiry Warning via Webhook and Response Header:
- Add TOKEN_EXPIRY_WARNING_SECONDS config (default 120)
- Add X-Token-Expires-In header on all authenticated responses
- Add X-Token-Refresh-Advised: true when remaining lifetime < threshold
- Emit token.expiring_soon webhook event (Redis-deduped per family_id)
- Emit token.refreshed webhook event after successful refresh grant

Tests: 357 unit tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
